matches.title: Multimodal Dynamics in Learning and Explaining Abstract and Concrete Concepts: A Triadic Interactive Task with Families
matches.abstract: Studies have shown that learning abstract concepts (e.g., freedom compared to table) may be particularly challenging. To address concept learning, most studies use paradigms involving a single participant, such as lexical decision, match to sample, induction, and rating tasks. Here, we use an interactive task involving three family members—mother, father, and a 5-year-old child—and examine how con

matches.title: Abstract Risk and the Politics of the Criminal Law
matches.abstract: Much of the criminal law contains what theorists call “abstract endangerment” statutes — crimes that punish not actual, but hypothetical, creation of risk. Consider the case of underage alcohol possession: age does not necessarily imply immaturity, and possession does not necessarily lead to consumption. The crime is therefore doubly “abstract”: many violations will create no risk of harm at all b

matches.title: Deviations in Distributions Perception through Imprecision and Accounting for Uncertainty through Abstract Representations
matches.abstract: Perceiving the distributions of values we experience accurately is crucial for adaptive judgment and decision making. Previous research shows that people are quite adept at learning distribution information, so that many models of judgment and decision making assume that people accurately store it. matches.title: Initial indications of generative AI influence in linguistics research abstracts: Evidence from a large-scale lexical frequency analysis between 2018 and 2025
matches.abstract: Generative AI and large language models (LLMs), such as ChatGPT, have transformed many working practices, including scientific writing. Previous large-scale studies have identified shifts in the use of certain stylistic markers, such as the word delve, in disciplines like biomedicine, engineering, and computer science, yet comparable evidence from the humanities remains scarce.
